post.summary:
CreateSchedule registers a new recurring run and registers its
cron runtime entry.
post.description:
The additional /v1/internal/schedules binding mirrors cr0n-a's
worker-facing route shape so the chunk-14 Python worker's
create_schedule MCP tool reuses cr0n's tool implementation
(modulo the body-shape remap from chunk 17).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateScheduleRequest is the request message for CreateSchedule.

get.summary: ListSchedules returns schedules visible to the caller.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description:
Optional owner filter. Implementations typically require a
non-admin caller to set this to their own user id.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description:
Optional enabled filter. When unset, both enabled and disabled
schedules are returned.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of schedules to return.

post.summary:
CreateSchedule registers a new recurring run and registers its
cron runtime entry.
post.description:
The additional /v1/internal/schedules binding mirrors cr0n-a's
worker-facing route shape so the chunk-14 Python worker's
create_schedule MCP tool reuses cr0n's tool implementation
(modulo the body-shape remap from chunk 17).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateScheduleRequest is the request message for CreateSchedule.

get.summary: GetSchedule reads a schedule by id.

delete.summary: DeleteSchedule removes the schedule and its cron runtime entry.

patch.summary:
UpdateSchedule modifies a schedule. Changes to `cron`,
`timezone`, or `enabled` MUST be reflected into the cron runtime
before the RPC returns.

cron.description:
Optional replacement cron expression. Implementations MUST
re-register the cron runtime when this field changes.

timezone.description:
Optional replacement IANA timezone. Implementations MUST
re-register the cron runtime when this field changes.

enabled.description:
Optional replacement enabled flag. On a true -> false transition
the cron runtime is suspended; on false -> true it is resumed.

sessionId.description:
Optional replacement session_id. An empty explicit string is a
distinct value from "leave unchanged"; use `set_session_id` to
disambiguate.

setSessionId.description: Whether `session_id` is being set (true) or left alone (false).

agentIds.description:
Optional replacement resource bindings. Sent in full; partial
merges are not supported. Paired with `set_*` flags so clearing
a binding is distinguishable from leaving it alone.

SchedulingUpdateScheduleBody.description:
UpdateScheduleRequest is the request message for UpdateSchedule.
Any field left unset is preserved.

userId.description:
Owning user. Implementations typically derive this from the
caller's auth principal; callers MAY set it explicitly when the
control plane is creating schedules on another user's behalf.

enabled.description:
Whether the schedule should fire immediately. Defaults to true
when unset; pass `enabled: false` explicitly to create a paused
schedule.

sessionId.description:
Optional session_id — if set, every fire continues the same
session.

v1CreateScheduleRequest.description: CreateScheduleRequest is the request message for CreateSchedule.

v1CreateScheduleResponse.description: CreateScheduleResponse is the response message for CreateSchedule.

v1DeleteScheduleResponse.description:
DeleteScheduleResponse is the response message for DeleteSchedule.
It is intentionally empty.

v1GetScheduleResponse.description: GetScheduleResponse is the response message for GetSchedule.

nextPageToken.description:
Opaque pagination token. Empty when there are no further
results.

v1ListSchedulesResponse.description: ListSchedulesResponse is the response message for ListSchedules.

userId.description:
Owning user. Fires are issued with `invoker_type = SCHEDULE`,
`invoker_id = <schedule id>`, `user_id = <this field>` — the
owning user's Config (agents, skills, connector tokens) is what
the fire runs under.

prompt.description: Task instruction. Sent verbatim to the agent on every fire.

cron.description:
5-field cron expression ("minute hour day-of-month month
day-of-week"). The spec standardizes on 5-field POSIX cron;
implementations MAY accept extensions (6-field with seconds,
predefined aliases like `@hourly`) as a best-effort convenience,
but `cron` round-trips through Update / Get without normalization.

timezone.description:
IANA Time Zone Database identifier (for example "UTC",
"America/New_York", "Asia/Tokyo"). The cron expression is
interpreted in this zone. Defaults to "UTC" when empty.

enabled.description:
When false, the implementation MUST NOT fire the schedule. On
transition to false the implementation SHOULD suspend its cron
runtime (for example set `suspend: true` on a Kubernetes CronJob)
rather than deleting it, so re-enabling is cheap.

sessionId.description:
Optional Workers session_id. When non-empty every fire of this
schedule reuses the same Nightshift session, so the agent sees
each fire as a continuation of a single multi-turn conversation.
Empty means every fire starts a fresh session.

agentIds.description:
Agent definitions (by Config.Agent id) to load on every fire.
The schedule stores ids only; Config resolves them into
AgentDefinitions at run start.

skillIds.description: Skill definitions (by Config.Skill id) to load on every fire.

connectorIds.description:
Connectors (by Config.Connector id) to authenticate on every
fire. At fire time, Secrets dispenses the owning user's tokens
for these connectors.

createdAt.description: Time the schedule was first created.

updatedAt.description: Time the schedule was most recently updated.

v1Schedule.description: Schedule is the declarative record of a recurring run.

v1UpdateScheduleResponse.description: UpdateScheduleResponse is the response message for UpdateSchedule.
